code:
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1★ (Very Negative):
    - Multi-purchase failures with safety hazards
    - Fundamental usability failures despite concept appeal
    - Negative updates invalidating previous satisfaction
    - Explicit "avoid purchase" warnings with defect evidence

    2★ (Negative):
    - Core functionality requires excessive workarounds
    - Persistent frustration despite occasional success
    - "Better alternatives exist" comparisons with justification
    - Regret expressed through cost/value complaints

    3★ (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with contextual limitations
    - Conditional recommendations ("works if...")
    - Adequate performance with notable compromises
    - Target-specific adequacy without endorsement

    4★ (Positive):
    - Single functional flaw impacting core utility
    - Brief factual positivity without enthusiasm
    - Lingering functional drawbacks post-resolution
    - Assembly difficulties despite final satisfaction
    - Explicit star deduction phrases ("only 4 because...")

    5★ (Very Positive):
    - Concise enthusiastic praise ("Works perfectly!") 
    - 3+ functional aspects praised despite 1 non-functional flaw
    - Close comparisons to top brands with satisfaction (+minor differences)
    - Long-term validation overcoming initial skepticism
    - Solves persistent problems exceeding expectations
    - Non-functional criticism WITH enthusiastic core praise
    - Core functionality excellence outweighing setup/learning curve

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - "Nearly matches X" →5 if satisfaction expressed regardless of superiority
    - Functional praise + non-functional criticism →5
    - Functional praise + functional flaw →4 (unless 3+ strengths)
    - Multi-purchase failures →1 regardless of initial experience
    - Brief effectiveness statements →4 unless enthusiastic
    - Setup difficulties →5 if core function flawless post-setup
    - Solving persistent problems from previous solutions →5 even with minor flaws
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "Second unit failed faster. Dangerous defects!"
<Reasoning>Multi-purchase safety failures override positives</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "Nearly matches Lodge quality! Daily driver despite minor differences"
<Reasoning>Close brand comparison with satisfaction regardless of slight inferiority</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "Roller hooks handle bumps perfectly! Installation took muscle."
<Reasoning>Core functionality excellence outweighs setup effort</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "Sharp tablecloth. Vibrant colors & durable - just needs ironing"
<Reasoning>Non-functional criticism with enthusiastic core praise</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "Works after 2 washes but edges stay stiff"
<Reasoning>Lingering functional drawback post-resolution</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "Solved our 5-year bump problem old hooks couldn't handle"
<Reasoning>Solves persistent problem exceeding previous solutions</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "Does the job adequately for light use"
<Reasoning>Brief factual statement lacking enthusiasm</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "Initially confusing buttons, now cleans flawlessly"
<Reasoning>Minor learning curve with core excellence</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "Good for guests but too flimsy for daily use"
<Reasoning>Contextual limitation with compromise</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 10:
Review: "Sturdy construction let down by recurring sizing issues"
<Reasoning>Functional flaw outweighs single strength</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>

{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (30 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 14
  gold 5 -> 4: 6
  gold 4 -> 3: 3
  gold 1 -> 2: 2
  gold 1 -> 3: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "This is a good, though not extra ordinary storage bin. It lacks exceptional build quality, exceptional attention to detail, or anything that's all that remarkable. It looks good, and the pattern is timeless, plus it keeps stuff together. You could do..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "I would have given this a five star rating had it fit the appliance it was purchased for. It didn't. With that being said, make sure you are positive of the model number of your ice tea maker before ordering and don't order based on how it "looks". H..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.33
  4. Review: "We own a huge amount of Lodge cast iron cook and bake ware plus a few odd pieces collected over the years. Although I've tried other brands in the past, Lodge remained the best. Now, I'd like to say this was equal or better than Lodge but it's not......"
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "There is only one problem for the experienced cook when it comes to this set - you're likely to already have one or more of these tools and possibly Oxo Good Grips at that! That's why I think this set is best suited for someone setting up a kitchen f..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "I like the Stasher Snack bag. It is the perfect size for snacks on the go. I also like that you can store, cook, put in the microwave or even in the dishwasher! This is great for small treats. Don't try to overstuff. You don't want the seal to get fo..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "This looks like a great idea and easy to use, but actually it's very awkward. The idea is to hang your jewelry in the closet. But the hanger part doesn't swivel, so if you want to select an item, you have to remove it from the closet and lay it on th..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 2 | Workers: [1, 2, 2] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "This was pretty straightforward to put together, feels very sturdy, leaves lots of room underneath for storage (plus a little space I left open because the dog seems to be happy about being able to curl up under there and use it as a hiding place), a..."
     Gold: 2 | Predicted: 2 | Workers: [2, 4, 2] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "Between a puppy and frequent visits from children, I've definitely had times when it felt like the gap between proper carpet cleanings was entirely too long. On the other hand, I never entirely bought the idea that a device of this type could turn ou..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I have only used this once so far, but I really like it. It is easy to operate once you figure out the steam buttons...lol! The scrubber was effective and it cleaned a pretty sticky kitchen floor. Next..on to my tile."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 30 errors, 22 borderline out of 80 examples."}
